title: phlox buckleyi
intro: Phlox buckleyi, also known as Wild Phlox or Wood Phlox, is a low-growing perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its fragrant pink or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Phlox buckleyi include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Phlox buckleyi typically involve making teas or infusions from the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
